r8PerformanceDepthMatrix.policy: R8 performance credibility must separate allocation-blocking REST and governance hot paths, promoted OpenAPI and governance latency, report-only HTTP latency, RPC unary and stream candidate evidence, and Gateway or Cache candidate report-only evidence before release notes compare gofly with Gin, Echo, Fiber, Hertz, Kitex, or gRPC-Go.

surfaces.evidenceState: tracked in baseline and current regression reports with allocs/op median blocking.
surfaces.rollbackAction: Revert route dispatch changes or pin the previous release when allocation budget evidence fails.

surfaces.evidenceState: tracked in baseline and current regression reports with path parameter allocation blocking.
surfaces.rollbackAction: Revert path extraction changes or pin the previous release when allocation budget evidence fails.

surfaces.evidenceState: tracked in baseline and current regression reports with JSON binding allocation blocking.
surfaces.rollbackAction: Revert binding or response changes until allocation budget evidence is restored.

surfaces.evidenceState: tracked in baseline and current regression reports with five-layer middleware allocation blocking.
surfaces.rollbackAction: Revert middleware chain changes or pin the previous release until allocation evidence recovers.

surfaces.evidenceState: tracked with five-sample baseline and promoted latency ratio for disabled OpenAPI metadata overhead.
surfaces.rollbackAction: Revert metadata path changes when disabled OpenAPI latency or allocation budgets fail.

surfaces.evidenceState: tracked with five-sample baseline and promoted latency ratio for enabled OpenAPI metadata overhead.
surfaces.rollbackAction: Revert schema or metadata path changes when OpenAPI latency or allocation budgets fail.

surfaces.evidenceState: tracked with five-sample baseline and promoted latency ratio for disabled governance overhead.
surfaces.rollbackAction: Revert request path changes when disabled governance latency or allocation budgets fail.

surfaces.evidenceState: tracked with five-sample baseline and promoted latency ratio for enabled governance rule matching.
surfaces.rollbackAction: Disable or revert the governance hot-path change until latency and allocation budgets recover.

surfaces.evidenceState: candidate evidence exists, but RPC unary stays outside trackedBenchmarks until allocation and latency promotion criteria pass.
surfaces.rollbackAction: Keep latency-critical methods on Kitex or existing gRPC paths until RPC Tier 1 evidence is promoted.

surfaces.evidenceState: aggregate stream governance remains candidate evidence until mode-specific baseline and current trend samples are published.
surfaces.rollbackAction: Keep stream workloads on existing gRPC or Kitex paths until stream governance budgets are promoted.

surfaces.evidenceState: server-stream governance remains candidate evidence until five-sample baseline and current trend samples are published.
surfaces.rollbackAction: Keep server-stream workloads on existing gRPC or Kitex paths until promoted budget evidence exists.

surfaces.evidenceState: client-stream governance remains candidate evidence until five-sample baseline and current trend samples are published.
surfaces.rollbackAction: Keep client-stream workloads on existing gRPC or Kitex paths until promoted budget evidence exists.

surfaces.evidenceState: bidirectional stream governance remains candidate evidence until five-sample baseline and current trend samples are published.
surfaces.rollbackAction: Keep bidirectional stream workloads on existing gRPC or Kitex paths until promoted budget evidence exists.

surfaces.evidenceState: dedicated gateway proxy benchmark evidence is published in bench/gateway_cache_bench_test.go and matrix.md, but it stays outside trackedBenchmarks until baseline, current trend, allocation budget, and rollback evidence are attached.
surfaces.rollbackAction: Keep gateway performance claims report-only and route traffic back to the previous gateway path when proxy benchmark evidence regresses before promotion.

surfaces.evidenceState: P17 promotes the in-memory cache Get hit path into tracked allocation blocking after five baseline samples, current trend evidence, zero allocation budget, and rollback guidance are attached.
surfaces.rollbackAction: Disable cache with GOFLY_CACHE_DISABLED or keep source-of-truth reads when the cache hot-path allocation budget regresses.

multiRunConfidence.policy: Promote new blocking performance claims only after the baseline has at least five samples, the current trend has at least three samples, allocation budgets pass, and latency rows have an explicit ratio and rollback action.

promotedAllocationBudgets.adopterAction: Block release when REST route allocation median exceeds the baseline ratchet tolerance.
promotedAllocationBudgets.rollbackAction: Revert the route hot-path change or keep the previous release until allocation evidence is restored.

promotedAllocationBudgets.adopterAction: Block release when path-parameter extraction allocation median exceeds the baseline ratchet tolerance.
promotedAllocationBudgets.rollbackAction: Revert the path-parameter change or keep the previous release until allocation evidence is restored.

promotedAllocationBudgets.adopterAction: Block release when JSON binding allocation median exceeds the baseline ratchet tolerance.
promotedAllocationBudgets.rollbackAction: Revert the binding change or keep the previous release until allocation evidence is restored.

promotedAllocationBudgets.adopterAction: Block release when middleware-chain allocation median exceeds the baseline ratchet tolerance.
promotedAllocationBudgets.rollbackAction: Revert the middleware change or keep the previous release until allocation evidence is restored.

promotedAllocationBudgets.adopterAction: Block release when OpenAPI-disabled metadata allocation median exceeds the baseline ratchet tolerance.
promotedAllocationBudgets.rollbackAction: Revert the metadata change or keep the previous release until allocation evidence is restored.

promotedAllocationBudgets.adopterAction: Block release when OpenAPI-enabled metadata allocation median exceeds the baseline ratchet tolerance.
promotedAllocationBudgets.rollbackAction: Revert the OpenAPI metadata change or keep the previous release until allocation evidence is restored.

promotedAllocationBudgets.adopterAction: Block release when governance-disabled allocation median exceeds the baseline ratchet tolerance.
promotedAllocationBudgets.rollbackAction: Revert the governance path change or keep the previous release until allocation evidence is restored.

promotedAllocationBudgets.adopterAction: Block release when governance-enabled allocation median exceeds the baseline ratchet tolerance.
promotedAllocationBudgets.rollbackAction: Revert the governance rule-match change or keep the previous release until allocation evidence is restored.

promotedAllocationBudgets.adopterAction: Block release when cache hot-path allocation median exceeds the zero-allocation baseline ratchet tolerance.
promotedAllocationBudgets.rollbackAction: Disable cache with GOFLY_CACHE_DISABLED or keep direct source-of-truth reads until allocation evidence is restored.

reportOnlyLatencyRows.promotionRequirement: Attach three current trend samples, maxRegressionRatio, reason, and rollback action before latency becomes blocking.
reportOnlyLatencyRows.adopterAction: Use latency as trend evidence only; do not block releases on this row until promotion criteria pass.

reportOnlyLatencyRows.promotionRequirement: Attach three current trend samples, maxRegressionRatio, reason, and rollback action before latency becomes blocking.
reportOnlyLatencyRows.adopterAction: Use latency as trend evidence only; do not block releases on this row until promotion criteria pass.

reportOnlyLatencyRows.promotionRequirement: Attach three current trend samples, maxRegressionRatio, reason, and rollback action before latency becomes blocking.
reportOnlyLatencyRows.adopterAction: Use latency as trend evidence only; do not block releases on this row until promotion criteria pass.

reportOnlyLatencyRows.promotionRequirement: Attach three current trend samples, maxRegressionRatio, reason, and rollback action before latency becomes blocking.
reportOnlyLatencyRows.adopterAction: Use latency as trend evidence only; do not block releases on this row until promotion criteria pass.

reportOnlyLatencyRows.promotionRequirement: Keep cache latency report-only until maxRegressionRatio, trend history, and cache-mode rollback evidence are documented.
reportOnlyLatencyRows.adopterAction: Use cache hot-path latency as trend evidence only; block releases only on allocs/op until latency promotion criteria pass.

candidateSurfaces.requiredEvidence: Keep the dedicated gateway proxy benchmark outside trackedBenchmarks until five baseline samples, three current trend samples, allocation budget, latency policy, and rollback guidance are attached.
candidateSurfaces.adopterAction: Use gateway proxy benchmark output as trend evidence only; do not claim ratcheted Gateway performance until promotion criteria pass.

candidateSurfaces.requiredEvidence: Keep the loader-hit cache benchmark outside trackedBenchmarks until it has separate five-sample baseline, current trend, cache-mode, and rollback evidence after the Get hit path promotion.
candidateSurfaces.adopterAction: Use loader-hit cache benchmark output as trend evidence only; the P17 blocking cache claim covers only BenchmarkCacheHotPath allocs/op.

adopterActions.trigger: Any tracked benchmark allocs/op median exceeds the ratchet tolerance under make bench-regression-check
adopterActions.action: Block the release, attach bench/regression-report.json, and revert or pin the previous release until allocation evidence is restored.

adopterActions.trigger: Any report-only latency trend regresses before promotion criteria and current trend samples are attached
adopterActions.action: Keep the release decision non-blocking, investigate the hot path, and require three-sample trend evidence before promoting the row.

adopterActions.trigger: gateway or cache performance is promoted from candidate evidence into blocking release gates
adopterActions.action: Require dedicated baseline, current trend, allocation budget, rollback evidence, and trackedBenchmarks promotion through bench-regression-check.

p9GatewayCacheOwnership.policy: Gateway and cache performance claims now have dedicated public benchmark rows, but they remain report-only until five baseline samples, three current trend samples, allocation budgets, latency policy, and rollback evidence are attached.

candidateSurfaces.baselineRequirement: minimum 5 baseline samples before allocation blocking promotion
candidateSurfaces.currentTrendRequirement: minimum 3 current trend samples before promotion review
candidateSurfaces.rollbackAction: Route traffic back to the previous gateway or keep gateway performance claims report-only when proxy benchmark evidence regresses.

candidateSurfaces.baselineRequirement: minimum 5 baseline samples before allocation blocking promotion
candidateSurfaces.currentTrendRequirement: minimum 3 current trend samples before promotion review
candidateSurfaces.rollbackAction: Disable cache with GOFLY_CACHE_DISABLED or keep direct source-of-truth reads when cache benchmark evidence regresses.

candidateSurfaces.rollbackAction: Keep cache loader-hit performance report-only until cache hit, miss, and invalidation evidence converge.

promotionCriteria: no allocation regression under bench-regression-check after promotion
latency remains report-only until maxRegressionRatio and rollback action are documented

p10PerformanceBudgetRatchet.policy: P10 keeps allocation budgets blocking for established REST/OpenAPI/governance hot paths, keeps weak-signal REST and RPC latency rows report-only, and forbids promoting gateway or cache benchmark claims until baseline and current trend confidence are attached.

blockingBudgets.confidence: five baseline samples with promoted latency maxRegressionRatio and rollback guidance
blockingBudgets.rollbackAction: Revert OpenAPI metadata-path changes or keep the previous release when latency or allocation budgets fail.

blockingBudgets.confidence: five baseline samples with promoted latency maxRegressionRatio and rollback guidance
blockingBudgets.rollbackAction: Revert OpenAPI schema or route metadata changes until benchmark evidence recovers.

blockingBudgets.confidence: five baseline samples with promoted latency maxRegressionRatio and rollback guidance
blockingBudgets.rollbackAction: Revert disabled-governance hot-path changes until benchmark evidence recovers.

blockingBudgets.confidence: five baseline samples with promoted latency maxRegressionRatio and rollback guidance
blockingBudgets.rollbackAction: Disable or revert governance rule-match changes until benchmark evidence recovers.

promotionHoldReasons.surface: REST latency except OpenAPI and governance
promotionHoldReasons.reason: Latency remains a weak signal until three current trend samples and a documented maxRegressionRatio exist for each row.
promotionHoldReasons.nextAction: Keep allocation blocking, publish trend evidence, and promote only one row at a time after stable multi-run confidence.

promotionHoldReasons.reason: RPC remains Tier 1 candidate evidence and has not completed the required release train or blocking budget promotion.
promotionHoldReasons.nextAction: Keep Kitex or existing gRPC rollback guidance attached and revisit after rpc-boundary-check plus bench-regression-check pass across release trains.

promotionHoldReasons.reason: Gateway proxy and cache loader-hit rows remain report-only, while P17 promotes only the in-memory cache Get hit path into allocation blocking.
promotionHoldReasons.nextAction: Keep Gateway and loader-hit candidates report-only, collect separate evidence, and do not promote additional Gateway or Cache rows without a new decision block.

promotionRules: latency rows require maxRegressionRatio and rollback action before blocking promotion
only one new latency surface should be promoted per governance round unless the evidence source is shared

adopterPerformanceContract.policy: Adopters may treat only ratcheted allocation budgets and explicitly promoted latency rows as blocking; RPC, gateway, cache loader-hit, and unpromoted HTTP latency rows remain trend evidence until promotion criteria pass.

blockingSurfaces.adopterAction: Block release when allocs/op median regresses beyond the ratchet tolerance for the REST route hot path.
blockingSurfaces.rollbackAction: Revert the route hot-path change or keep the previous release until allocation evidence is restored.

blockingSurfaces.adopterAction: Block release when promoted governance rule-match latency or allocation budgets regress under bench-regression-check.
blockingSurfaces.rollbackAction: Disable the new governance hot-path change or keep the previous policy implementation until benchmark evidence recovers.

blockingSurfaces.adopterAction: Block release when cache hot-path allocs/op median regresses beyond the zero-allocation ratchet tolerance.
blockingSurfaces.rollbackAction: Disable cache with GOFLY_CACHE_DISABLED or keep direct source-of-truth reads until allocation evidence is restored.

reportOnlySurfaces.adopterAction: Use unpromoted HTTP latency rows as trend evidence, not release blockers, until the ratchet promotes a row.
reportOnlySurfaces.rollbackAction: Investigate trend regressions before promotion and keep allocation budgets as the blocking guardrail.

reportOnlySurfaces.adopterAction: Treat RPC rows as candidate trend evidence and keep latency-critical methods on Kitex or existing gRPC paths.
reportOnlySurfaces.rollbackAction: Block RPC Tier 1 promotion until release trains and blocking budget evidence are attached.

reportOnlySurfaces.adopterAction: Treat Gateway proxy and cache loader-hit rows as candidate trend evidence until separate baseline, current trend, allocation budget, and rollback guidance are attached.
reportOnlySurfaces.rollbackAction: Keep Gateway proxy or loader-hit performance claims report-only and use existing functional smoke gates when candidate benchmark evidence regresses before promotion.

promotionRules: latency rows promoted only after documented reason and maxRegressionRatio
rollback guidance remains attached for candidate RPC rows

capture.currentSourcePolicy: bench/current.txt is ignored local runtime evidence; committed promotion decisions must use this machine-readable attachment and must not commit local benchmark output.

decision.reason: P16 attaches a five-sample Gateway and Cache current trend, but keeps promotion held because committed baseline rows, allocation budgets, cache-mode notes, and release rollback evidence are not all attached together.
decision.releaseNotePolicy: Release notes may mention the P16 Gateway and Cache current trend as report-only evidence, but must not claim allocation-blocking coverage, blocking latency, or production performance parity until baseline and promotion criteria pass.

currentTrendRows.cacheModeNote: gateway proxy benchmark exercises httptest upstream proxying and remains a Gateway candidate, not a Cache mode claim
currentTrendRows.rollbackAction: Keep gateway proxy performance claims report-only and route traffic through the previous gateway path when candidate benchmark evidence regresses.

currentTrendRows.cacheModeNote: cache hit path measures in-memory L1 Get hit behavior with no loader execution
currentTrendRows.rollbackAction: Keep cache performance claims report-only, disable cache with GOFLY_CACHE_DISABLED, or use source-of-truth reads when candidate benchmark evidence regresses.

currentTrendRows.cacheModeNote: cache GetOrLoad hit path measures loader-backed key after warmup and does not exercise miss or invalidation behavior
currentTrendRows.rollbackAction: Keep cache loader-hit performance report-only until cache hit, miss, and invalidation benchmark evidence converge.

attachmentRules: P16 current trend attachment must include exactly five samples for each Gateway and Cache candidate row
P16 current trend attachment must not add Gateway or Cache rows to trackedBenchmarks
P16 current trend attachment must keep Gateway and Cache latency report-only

decision.reason: P16 allocation promotion review keeps Gateway and Cache out of blocking coverage because current trend samples exist but committed baseline rows are still missing, so allocation deltas cannot be compared by bench-regression-check.
decision.releaseNotePolicy: Release notes may describe the P16 Gateway and Cache allocation promotion review as a hold decision with report-only trend evidence, but must not claim allocation-blocking coverage, blocking latency, or production performance parity until committed baseline rows pass the gate.

blockers: committed five-sample Cache baseline row is not published

trackedBenchmarks entry would create a false blocking signal

reviewedSurfaces.rollbackGuardrail: Keep cache hit-path benchmark claims report-only, use GOFLY_CACHE_DISABLED for runtime rollback, and promote only after baseline evidence passes bench-regression-check.

blockers: committed five-sample loader-hit baseline row is not published

blockingRules: P16 allocation promotion review must not add Gateway or Cache rows to trackedBenchmarks
P16 allocation promotion review must keep Gateway and Cache latency report-only

P16 allocation promotion review requires committed five-sample baseline rows before allocation blocking
P16 allocation promotion review requires rollback guardrails and cache-mode notes before promotion
P16 allocation promotion review requires bench-regression-check before any trackedBenchmarks update

capture.command: GOCACHE=/private/tmp/gofly-p17-gocache GOTMPDIR=/private/tmp/gofly-p17-gotmp BENCH_PATTERN='BenchmarkGatewayProxy|BenchmarkCacheHotPath' BENCH_COUNT=5 BENCH_PKGS='./bench/' BENCH_DIR=/private/tmp/gofly-p17-bench bash bin/scripts/benchstat.sh --baseline
capture.baselineSourcePolicy: Gateway/Cache targeted baseline was captured in an isolated temporary bench directory so the existing tracked REST baseline file remains unchanged.

decision.reason: P17 attaches five-sample Gateway and Cache baseline summaries without promoting them because the tracked regression file still excludes these candidate rows and P17-03 must make the explicit one-surface allocation promotion decision.
decision.releaseNotePolicy: Release notes may describe the P17 Gateway and Cache baseline attachment as report-only baseline evidence, but must not claim allocation-blocking coverage, blocking latency, or production performance parity until P17 promotion criteria pass.

baselineRows.cacheModeNote: gateway proxy benchmark covers HTTP proxy overhead and remains separate from cache hit-path semantics
baselineRows.rollbackAction: Keep gateway proxy benchmark claims report-only until P17-03 selects one surface and bench-regression-check validates the allocation budget.

baselineRows.cacheModeNote: cache hot-path benchmark measures in-memory L1 Get hit behavior and does not cover misses or invalidation
baselineRows.rollbackAction: Keep cache hit-path benchmark claims report-only, use GOFLY_CACHE_DISABLED for runtime rollback, and promote only after P17-03 selects the budget surface.

baselineRows.cacheModeNote: cache GetOrLoad hit benchmark measures a warmed loader-backed key and does not prove miss or invalidation behavior
baselineRows.rollbackAction: Keep loader-hit benchmark claims report-only and require separate cache hit, miss, and invalidation evidence before any blocking promotion.

attachmentRules: P17 baseline attachment must include exactly five samples for each Gateway and Cache candidate row
P17 baseline attachment must not add Gateway or Cache rows to trackedBenchmarks
P17 baseline attachment must keep Gateway and Cache latency report-only
P17 baseline attachment must keep promotion blocked until P17-03 selects at most one allocation surface
P17 baseline attachment must not overwrite the existing tracked REST baseline artifact with a Gateway/Cache-only baseline

decision.reason: P17 promotes only BenchmarkCacheHotPath because it has five baseline samples, current trend evidence, zero B/op, zero allocs/op, a narrow in-memory Get hit-path scope, and explicit cache rollback guidance; Gateway proxy and loader-hit rows remain broader candidate evidence.
decision.releaseNotePolicy: Release notes may describe BenchmarkCacheHotPath allocs/op as allocation-blocking evidence, but must not claim blocking cache latency, Gateway performance parity, loader-hit coverage, cache miss coverage, invalidation coverage, or production performance parity until separate promotion criteria pass.

selectedBudget.cacheModeNote: This promoted budget covers only the in-memory L1 Get hit path and does not cover loader hits, misses, invalidation, remote cache, or eviction behavior.
selectedBudget.rollbackAction: Set GOFLY_CACHE_DISABLED=true, bypass the cache layer, or keep direct source-of-truth reads until BenchmarkCacheHotPath allocation evidence returns to zero.

blockers: gateway proxy is a broader HTTP proxy path and should not share the cache hot-path promotion

latency remains report-only and needs a separate maxRegressionRatio before any blocking claim
heldRows.rollbackAction: Keep Gateway proxy performance claims report-only and route traffic through the previous gateway path when benchmark evidence regresses.

blockers: loader-hit behavior is distinct from plain Get hit behavior and needs its own promotion decision
cache miss and invalidation behavior remain outside this benchmark row
latency remains report-only and needs separate cache-mode rollback evidence before promotion
heldRows.rollbackAction: Keep loader-hit performance claims report-only and require separate hit, miss, invalidation, and loader fallback evidence before blocking promotion.

promotionRules: P17 may promote exactly one Gateway or Cache allocation surface
P17 promoted Cache row must be present in trackedBenchmarks
P17 promoted Cache row must be present in bench/baseline.txt
P17 promoted Cache row must stay report-only for latency
P17 Gateway proxy and loader-hit rows must stay out of trackedBenchmarks
P17 release notes must not claim production Gateway or Cache performance parity

decision.reason: P18 keeps BenchmarkGatewayProxy outside blocking allocation coverage because the row is stable enough for trend visibility but still carries 154 allocs/op on the proxy path, which is too high for a release-blocking Gateway claim without a lower target and rollback boundary.
decision.releaseNotePolicy: Release notes may describe BenchmarkGatewayProxy as Gateway proxy report-only evidence, but must not claim allocation-blocking Gateway claim, blocking Gateway latency, Gateway performance parity, or production Gateway performance parity until the allocation target and rollback boundary pass.

holdBudget.targetRationale: Gateway proxy promotion requires a measurable allocation reduction below the current 154 allocs/op baseline before the row can become a release blocker.
holdBudget.rollbackBoundary: Keep Gateway proxy claims report-only and route traffic through the previous gateway path when current trend evidence exceeds baseline or the allocation target is missed.

blockingRules: P18 Gateway proxy must stay out of trackedBenchmarks
P18 Gateway proxy latency must remain report-only
P18 Gateway proxy promotion requires allocation target clearance
P18 Gateway proxy promotion requires rollback boundary evidence
P18 Gateway proxy release notes must forbid Gateway performance parity claims

decision.reason: P18 keeps BenchmarkCacheHotPathGetOrLoadHit outside blocking allocation coverage because the row proves only a warmed loader-backed hit and does not prove cache miss behavior, invalidation correctness, or loader fallback recovery under failure.
decision.releaseNotePolicy: Release notes may describe BenchmarkCacheHotPathGetOrLoadHit as loader-hit report-only evidence, but must not claim loader-hit allocation-blocking claim, blocking cache loader-hit latency, cache miss coverage, invalidation coverage, or production cache performance parity until missing evidence passes.

precheckBudget.scopeBoundary: This precheck covers only warmed GetOrLoad hits and must not be merged with the promoted plain Get hit-path budget.
precheckBudget.rollbackBoundary: Keep loader-hit claims report-only and use GOFLY_CACHE_DISABLED or direct source-of-truth reads until miss and invalidation evidence converge.

missingEvidence.requiredBenchmark: cache miss benchmark with loader execution
missingEvidence.blockingReason: Loader-hit evidence does not prove first-load miss allocation or latency behavior.

missingEvidence.requiredBenchmark: cache invalidation benchmark with refreshed value
missingEvidence.blockingReason: Loader-hit evidence does not prove stale value eviction or invalidation cost.

missingEvidence.requiredBenchmark: loader fallback benchmark with error and recovery paths
missingEvidence.blockingReason: Loader-hit evidence does not prove fallback behavior when the loader fails.

blockingRules: P18 loader-hit must stay out of trackedBenchmarks

P18 loader-hit promotion requires cache miss evidence
P18 loader-hit promotion requires invalidation evidence
P18 loader-hit promotion requires loader fallback evidence

decision.releaseNotePolicy: Release notes must keep RPC Tier 1 promotion blocked and must not claim Kitex transport parity, gRPC-Go ecosystem parity, blocking RPC latency, or drop-in RPC replacement readiness.

blockers: release-train evidence is not attached
latest allocation evidence is not stable enough for blocking promotion
latency remains report-only without two-release trend confidence
candidateRows.rollbackAction: Keep latency-critical unary methods on Kitex or native gRPC-Go until unary allocation evidence is stable enough for blocking promotion.

blockers: release-train evidence is not attached
server-stream five-sample baseline is not published
server-stream current trend is not published
candidateRows.rollbackAction: Keep server-stream workloads on Kitex or native gRPC-Go until server-stream allocation evidence is stable.

blockers: release-train evidence is not attached
client-stream five-sample baseline is not published
client-stream current trend is not published
candidateRows.rollbackAction: Keep upload-style client streams on the previous RPC stack until client-stream budget evidence is promoted.

blockers: release-train evidence is not attached
bidi-stream five-sample baseline is not published
bidi-stream current trend is not published
candidateRows.rollbackAction: Keep realtime bidirectional sessions on Kitex or native gRPC-Go until bidirectional stream evidence is promoted.

blockingRules: exactly one RPC surface may be promoted at a time
promoted RPC rows must enter trackedBenchmarks only through bench-regression-check
promoted RPC rows require minimum 5 baseline samples
promoted RPC rows require minimum 3 current trend samples
promoted RPC rows require no allocation regression under bench-regression-check
promoted RPC rows require rollback guidance naming Kitex, native gRPC-Go, or the previous RPC stack
RPC latency remains report-only until maxRegressionRatio and two-release trend evidence are documented

decision.reason: P14 does not add RPC benchmarks to trackedBenchmarks because no release train includes complete RPC boundary, release evidence, required-check drift, benchmark trend, allocation budget, and rollback evidence together.
